title: carex digitata var. subnivalis
intro: Carex digitata var. subnivalis, also known as the subalpine sedge, is a type of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The subalpine sedge has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, treating digestive issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
